summary: End-to-end frontend test of the Evangelion-themed NERV portfolio (static frontend, no backend). All 14 review-request scenarios verified successfully on the public preview URL: boot sequence + skip, localStorage persistence (nerv_boot_done_v1, nerv_pro_mode, nerv_sound, nerv_unlocked), hero, full nav (9 buttons), mode toggle (adds pro-mode class on <html>), sound toggle, operations modal open/close, secret terminal (~ toggle + help/whoami/thirdimpact/magi/instrumentality commands), NERV-logo 10x click -> third impact overlay, Konami code -> SEELE overlay, contact form (UI gracefully shows TRANSMISSION COMPLETE despite expected Web3Forms CORS failure), resume PDF download (/resume.pdf returns 200, application/pdf, 685 bytes), terminate-session -> ending-screen.

integration_issues.issue: Web3Forms request blocked by CORS (Access-Control-Allow-Origin missing) with placeholder access_key 'REPLACE_WITH_WEB3FORMS_KEY'. This is expected per problem statement; UI handles failure gracefully and still logs 'TRANSMISSION COMPLETE' in the terminal log. Will need a real access_key configured before production.

action_items: Replace Web3Forms placeholder access_key with a real key for production deployment (currently 'REPLACE_WITH_WEB3FORMS_KEY' — submits fail with CORS, but UI is resilient).
Optional: silence the console CORS error on submit by adding mode: 'no-cors' or wrapping fetch in a try/catch that does not log to console (current behavior still works for UX).
critical_code_review_comments: App.js (213 lines) is approaching the soft size threshold; consider extracting Konami/easter-egg keyboard handlers and overlay routing into a custom hook for readability.
Two separate `keydown` listeners are attached in App.js (one for terminal/Konami/escape, one for ascii easter eggs). They can be consolidated into a single handler to reduce listener overhead.
Boot sequence uses many setTimeouts; OK for one-shot but if user spam-skips quickly, the unmount cleanup correctly clears them (verified).
Random angel popup interval (65s, 50% chance) fires while overlays are visible; current code guards with `o ?? 'angel'` so it won't replace, good.
Contact form: catching Web3Forms failure and still showing 'TRANSMISSION COMPLETE' may mislead a real user — consider showing a non-blocking warning when access_key is the placeholder.
test_credentials: N/A — no auth in this static app
context_for_next_testing_agent: App is a pure static frontend at https://nerv-archives-aarav.preview.emergentagent.com . All data-testids referenced in the request exist and work as documented. Boot is gated by localStorage key 'nerv_boot_done_v1' — clear localStorage to retest boot. Contact submission to Web3Forms fails with CORS due to placeholder access_key, but UI logs success anyway (intentional). Resume PDF is a valid 685-byte %PDF-1.4 file served at /resume.pdf with Content-Type application/pdf. No backend endpoints to test.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. The Web3Forms CORS error in console is by-design due to placeholder access_key and is non-blocking.
